steps.title: Create two points
steps.content: The point $(0,2)$ is on the line. A slope of $\frac{1}{3}$ means moving $3$ units right raises $y$ by $1$, so another point is $(3,3)$. Enter those two points into a Desmos table.

steps.title: Run linear regression
steps.content: Use $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=0.333333...$ and $b=2$, so the equation is $f(x)=\frac{1}{3}x+2$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice A matches the fitted slope and intercept. Choice B has intercept $\frac{1}{2}$, choice C has intercept $-\frac{2}{3}$, and choice D has intercept $-2$, so those fail the point $(0,2)$. Choice A is correct.
